summary: Markets are cautiously optimistic amid geopolitical tensions from US/Iran developments, with oil surging above $115/bbl while equities face macro headwinds from rising rates and energy prices. Safe-haven flows are supporting the dollar and gold, though risk sentiment remains fragile as traders navigate central bank policy uncertainty.

signals.name: Bitcoin
signals.reason: Daily and weekly VWAP remain bullish with price above 82.5k target, though geopolitical risk and macro uncertainty create volatility.

signals.name: Ethereum
signals.reason: Trading above EMA50 with continued positive momentum and bullish trendline support, RSI indicators showing room for further gains.

signals.name: Gold
signals.reason: Gold bounced toward $4,550/oz on safe-haven demand but remains in short-term downtrend targeting $4,466–$4,423 with bearish technical signals.

signals.name: Brent Crude Oil
signals.reason: Brent surged above $115/bbl driven by US/Iran geopolitical tensions, though elevated volatility and macro uncertainty present significant downside risk.

signals.name: EUR/USD
signals.reason: Euro strengthened on positive Eurozone data but is testing support B of 1.1687–1.1670 for the third time with mixed technical signals.

signals.name: USD/JPY
signals.reason: USD/JPY hovering above 157 near 100-day moving average, supported by rising Treasury yields and safe-haven dollar demand despite recent intervention.

signals.name: USD/CHF
signals.reason: Attempting to correct downtrend at key resistance 0.7840 but facing negative pressure trading below EMA50, limiting upside potential.

signals.name: AUD/USD
signals.reason: Australian Dollar showing resilience supported by commodity price increases and improved trade relations with Asia.

signals.name: USD/CAD
signals.reason: Canadian Dollar under pressure due to fluctuating oil prices impacting commodity-linked currency sentiment.

signals.name: Copper
signals.reason: Declined below $5.81 support with stochastic negativity targeting $5.70, bearish corrective trend expected to reach $5.59–$5.51.
